message.content:
@startuml
left to right direction
actor "User Agent" as UA
interface "Content removal API" as API
interface "User Manager" as UM
database "User database" as UD
database "Content database" as CD

note right of UA
  Should also include a CSRF token to prevent admins who are also
  visit other, potentially XSS vulnerable or malicious sites
  from helping an unknown actor remove content without authorization.
end note

UA -> API: Removal request (HTTP POST),\ncontains ID of content to be removed.

activate API
API -> UM: Retrieve role of requesting user.
activate UM
UM -> UD: Fetch user information from database.
activate UD
UD --> UM: Return query result.
deactivate UD
UM --> API: Return user role.
deactivate UM

alt [User has admin role]
  activate API
  API -> API: Validate removal request
  alt [Failed to validate removal request]
    API --> UA: Return an HTTP status code that signals validation failure.\nExact value depends on how the validation failed.
  else [Request is deemed valid]
    API -> CD: Remove content from DB.
    activate CD
    CD --> API: Result of delete operation
    deactivate CD
    alt [Content has been removed succesfully]
      API --> UA: Return HTTP 204 No Content
    else [Removal failed]
      API --> UA: Return HTTP Status code to signal reason for failure.
    end
  end
  deactivate API
else [User is not authorized to remove content]
  API --> UA: Return HTTP 403 Forbidden to signal that the user has no permission to request what they just did.
end

deactivate API

note right of UA
  Should also include a CSRF token to prevent admins who are also
  visit other, potentially XSS vulnerable or malicious sites
  from helping an unknown actor remove content without authorization.
end note

@enduml
